_meta.purpose: formal gate and PR-opening rules. Feature: the canonical PR opens only after a versioned APPROVED. Fix: the PR opens by human authorization, without a formal review.

review_preconditions: a branch with an effective diff exists for the item
all tasks with status=DONE in status.yaml, with commit and evidence filled in (inv-03)
build and tests executed on the branch
pr_open_preconditions: the reviewer issued Decision=APPROVED in revision-X.md
status.yaml remains at current_phase=revision and current_agent=reviewer until the PR is effectively opened
branch with its diff reconciled against tasks[].commit (inv-06)

pr_open_confirmation.reviewer_prompt: after versioning revision-X.md with Decision=APPROVED, ask the human in chat whether to open the PR or whether there are further adjustments, in a single objective message per transitions.json > communication_contract
pr_open_confirmation.note: the conversational confirmation does not change the state machine; it only authorizes the effective opening of the PR

assembly: Consolidated summary of tasks[].evidence from status.yaml
when there are corrective rounds N>1, replace the tasks[].evidence summary with the Correction Evidence (Self-Report) of the approved revision-N.md
pr_description_source.note: tasks.md contains no evidence; evidence lives exclusively in status.yaml

rejected: revision-X.md contains the review + Non-Conformities + Corrective Handoff + Required Actions
status.yaml transitions directly to implementing with current_agent=implementer, recovery.active_artifact=revisions/revision-X.md and recovery.active_task=revision-X
commit and push of the versioned changes to revisions/ and status.yaml
approved: revision-X.md with Decision=APPROVED committed before the PR is opened
the reviewer confirms in chat whether to open the PR or whether there are further adjustments, in a single objective message
after an affirmative authorization: open the PR, fill status.yaml.pr.number/url/created_at and only then update phases[revision].result=APPROVED and current_phase=done
commit and push of the versioned changes to revisions/ and status.yaml

closure_checklist: acceptance criteria of all tasks verified
build and tests passing without blocking failures
tasks[].commit and tasks[].evidence filled in for all DONE tasks
all applicable invariants from transitions.json validated
status.yaml.pr.number, pr.url and pr.created_at filled in
docs updated when the feature changes the operating model or workflow
AI prompts and contracts updated when there is AI impact

review_preconditions: the fix/<suffix> branch exists with an effective diff
the human copilot declared explicit closure of the bundle
the retrospective fix.md was created in agents/fixes/<branch-suffix>/ with Objective, Files Touched and Evidence filled in
pr_open_preconditions: explicit human authorization in chat (no revision-X.md, no formal reviewer)
fix.md committed on the branch before the PR is opened

pr_open_confirmation.fixer_prompt: after committing fix.md, ask the human in a single objective message whether to open the PR or whether there are further adjustments

assembly: when the human did not request a formal fix.md, the description uses only the git log + 1-2 lines of context

lightweight_fix_gate.stop_and_ask_remind: before any commit that crosses a stop_and_ask_gate (methodology.md §8.2 / roles/fixer.json), the fixer asks the human and awaits a reply. A DB reset or equivalent destructive action requires a prior warning (methodology.md §1.5).

merge_rules: formal approval and merge are distinct events
the final merge happens via squash commit, a manual human action
formal closure happens before the merge, still on the branch
do not depend on merged_at or auto-merge to close formally
